title: ChatHouseDiffusion: Prompt-Guided Generation and Editing of Floor Plans
abstract: Combines LLMs for natural language processing, graphormer for encoding spatial relationships, and diffusion models for flexible floor plan generation and editing. Enables iterative design adjustments and achieves higher IoU scores than existing approaches.
method_summary: Three-component architecture: LLMs interpret user instructions, graphormer encodes topological relationships, diffusion models generate and edit layouts supporting interactive localized modifications.

title: HouseTune: LLM-Assisted Two-Phase Text-to-Floorplan Generation
abstract: Two-stage text-to-floorplan framework combining LLM reasoning with diffusion model refinement. First stage uses Chain-of-Thought prompting for initial layout, second stage applies conditional diffusion for geometric precision and structural detail.
method_summary: LLM reasoning for intuitive layout generation followed by diffusion-based refinement for geometric precision, reducing learning difficulty without extensive domain-specific training data.

title: HouseMind: Tokenization Allows Multimodal Large Language Models to Understand, Generate and Edit Architectural Floor Plans
abstract: Multimodal LLM unifying floor plan understanding, generation, and editing. Introduces discrete room-instance tokens via VQ-VAE to construct unified vocabulary bridging layouts and symbolic reasoning. Achieves superior geometric validity and controllability.
method_summary: Discrete room-instance tokens as unified vocabulary for floor plans, combined with multimodal alignment and instruction tuning for coherent controllable layout generation from text.

title: HouseDiffusion: Vector Floorplan Generation via a Diffusion Model with Discrete and Continuous Denoising
abstract: Diffusion-based approach for generating vector floorplans by denoising 2D room/door corner coordinates. Uses continuous noise inversion and discrete coordinate prediction for geometric relationships. Transformer with graph-conditioned attention masks. Outperforms prior work on RPLAN.
method_summary: Transformer with dual denoising objectives (continuous for coordinate precision, discrete for geometric constraints) and graph-conditioned attention masks producing vector-graphics floorplans as 1D polygonal loops.

title: GSDiff: Synthesizing Vector Floorplans via Geometry-enhanced Structural Graph Generation
abstract: Generative framework for vector floorplan design via structural graph generation focusing on wall junction generation and wall segment prediction. Introduces alignment loss and random self-supervision to reduce geometric inconsistencies.
method_summary: Two-stage structural graph generation: (1) wall junction generation with alignment loss, (2) wall segment prediction with random self-supervision, combining diffusion models with Transformers.

title: Generating Floorplans for Various Building Functionalities via Latent Diffusion Model
abstract: Generative latent diffusion model creating floorplans for different building types based on building footprints and design briefs. Learns from complexity of inter-connections between diverse building types.
method_summary: Latent diffusion model trained on diverse building types to generate novel floorplan configurations from footprints and design briefs, producing new combinations blending architectural elements.

title: Text2CAD: Text to 3D CAD Generation via Technical Drawings
abstract: Framework converting textual descriptions into isometric images, then transforming into orthographic views to reconstruct 3D CAD models while maintaining physical consistency.
method_summary: Stable diffusion models translate text descriptions into isometric technical drawings, subsequently converted into orthographic projections for 3D model reconstruction.

title: Drawing2CAD: Sequence-to-Sequence Learning for CAD Generation from Vectorized Drawings
abstract: Converts 2D engineering drawings into parametric CAD models via sequence-to-sequence learning. Introduces vector representation system, dual-decoder transformer, soft target loss, and CAD-VGDrawing paired dataset.
method_summary: Three components: (1) network-friendly vector representation, (2) dual-decoder transformer decoupling command type from parameter generation, (3) soft target distribution loss for parameter flexibility.

title: Component Segmentation of Engineering Drawings Using Graph Convolutional Networks
abstract: Data-driven framework automating vectorization and machine interpretation of 2D engineering part drawings. Converts raster images into vectorized components, constructs connectivity graph, and applies GCN to classify semantic types (text, dimensions, contours).
method_summary: Image processing (thinning, stroke tracing, cubic Bézier fitting) for vectorization, graph construction for component connectivity, GCN for semantic label prediction, overcoming pixel sparsity limitations.

title: A Comprehensive End-to-End Computer Vision Framework for Restoration and Recognition of Low-Quality Engineering Drawings
abstract: End-to-end system for degraded engineering drawing digitization. K-means clustering for patch classification, modified ESRGAN and Faster R-CNN for enhancement and recognition, task-driven collaborative learning. Achieves 98.98% accuracy and 99.33% recall on electrical diagrams.
method_summary: K-means clustering segments patches; ESRGAN restores quality; Faster R-CNN identifies symbols; multi-stage collaborative learning aligns restoration with recognition; synthetic data generation supports training.

title: Advanced Knowledge Extraction of Physical Design Drawings, Translation and Conversion to CAD Formats using Deep Learning
abstract: Deep learning pipeline for digitizing physical design drawings into CAD formats. Combines YOLOv7/Faster R-CNN for element detection, Canny filter for line extraction, OCR for text capture, and CSV consolidation for automated CAD conversion.
method_summary: Object detection models identify drawing components, edge and curve detection extract geometric features, OCR recognizes text, consolidated data structures enable automated CAD conversion.

title: From Engineering Diagrams to Graphs: Digitizing P&IDs with Transformers
abstract: Transformer-based approach using Relationformer to jointly extract symbols and connections from P&IDs. First public benchmark dataset for P&ID digitization with graph-level annotations. Over 25% improvement in edge detection accuracy vs modular baselines.
method_summary: Unified transformer architecture for joint symbol extraction and relationship detection, moving beyond traditional decomposed pipelines for better structural information capture.

title: Digitize-PID: Automatic Digitization of Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
abstract: End-to-end pipeline for automated P&ID digitization. Novel kernel-based line detection and two-step deep recognition for complex symbols. Created Dataset-P&ID with 500 synthetic annotated diagrams. Validated on synthetic and real-world datasets.
method_summary: Three stages: (1) kernel-based line identification and deep learning symbol recognition, (2) element association, (3) domain knowledge validation and correction.

title: OSSR-PID: One-Shot Symbol Recognition in P&ID Sheets using Path Sampling and GCN
abstract: One-shot learning for P&ID symbol recognition. Samples pixels along contour boundaries to construct graphs, uses Dynamic Graph CNN with ResNet-34 embeddings and Arcface loss. Strong results on synthetic and real-world P&ID datasets using only one example per class.
method_summary: Contour-based path sampling with graph construction feeding into DGCNN trained with Arcface loss, augmented by ResNet-34 features for discriminative one-shot symbol embeddings.

title: An Agentic Approach to Automatic Creation of P&ID Diagrams from Natural Language Descriptions
abstract: AI-driven copilot generating P&ID diagrams from natural language input. Multi-step agentic workflow leveraging LLMs and VLMs for iterative diagram generation, improving over zero-shot and few-shot baselines.
method_summary: Multi-step agentic workflow with LLMs and VLMs providing iterative structured process for P&ID generation from natural language, evaluated on workflow soundness and completeness.

title: Talking like Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs)
abstract: Enables natural language interaction with P&IDs by representing them as labeled property graphs through DEXPI data model and integrating with LLMs. Three-part approach: pyDEXPI conversion, knowledge graph construction, and graph-RAG for conversational queries.
method_summary: Converts P&IDs from DEXPI format into graph representations, constructs knowledge graphs, and integrates with LLMs through graph-RAG for natural language queries and contextual retrieval.

title: Visual Language Model as a Judge for Object Detection in Industrial Diagrams
abstract: Uses Visual Language Models to identify missing or inconsistent detections in industrial diagrams like P&IDs, enabling automated quality assessment and improving overall detection performance.
method_summary: VLM multimodal capabilities evaluate object detection outputs, identify detection gaps, and guide refinement for industrial diagram digitalization.

title: Advanced Integration of Discrete Line Segments in Digitized P&ID for Continuous Instrument Connectivity
abstract: Addresses P&ID digitization by merging line segments detected via computer vision to establish connections between equipment and instruments, enabling storage in knowledge graphs for route optimization and cycle detection.
method_summary: Merges detected line segments from CV models and builds connections between equipment and merged lines for comprehensive digitized P&ID representation.

title: SINA: A Circuit Schematic Image-to-Netlist Generator Using Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Open-source system for converting circuit schematic images into machine-readable netlists. Achieves 96.47% netlist-generation accuracy, substantially outperforming existing approaches.
method_summary: Combines deep learning for component detection, Connected-Component Labeling for connectivity extraction, OCR for reference designator retrieval, and VLM for reliable designator assignments.

title: AMSnet 2.0: A Large AMS Database with AI Segmentation for Net Detection
abstract: Addresses limitations in multimodal LLMs' ability to parse circuit schematics. Introduces AI-based segmentation for robust net detection. Expands AMSnet to 2,686 circuits with schematic images, netlists, digital schematics, and component positioning.
method_summary: AI-based segmentation replacing hard-coded heuristics for robust net detection in circuit schematics, enabling recovery of spatial information and digital reconstruction.

title: Masala-CHAI: A Large-Scale SPICE Netlist Dataset for Analog Circuits by Harnessing AI
abstract: Automated framework using LLMs to generate SPICE netlists from analog circuit schematics. Three-step workflow: circuit labeling, prompt tuning, verification. 7,500 schematics from 10 textbooks. Fine-tuned models achieve 46% improvement in Pass@1 scores with AnalogCoder.
method_summary: Multimodal LLM capabilities (GPT-4) convert circuit schematic images into SPICE netlists through automated labeling, systematic prompt engineering, and netlist verification.

title: OmniSch: A Multimodal PCB Schematic Benchmark For Structured Diagram Visual Reasoning
abstract: Benchmark for evaluating large multimodal models on PCB schematic understanding. 1,854 real-world diagrams with four task categories: visual grounding (109.9K instances), diagram-to-graph reasoning, geometric reasoning, and tool-augmented agentic reasoning.
method_summary: Comprehensive evaluation framework combining visual grounding, topological graph construction, geometric weight assignment, and agent-based visual search for schematic-to-netlist conversion.

title: SkeySpot: Automating Service Key Detection for Digital Electrical Layout Plans in the Construction Industry
abstract: Automated detection of electrical symbols in scanned floor plans. Dataset of 45 digitized electrical layout plans with 2,450 annotated instances across 34 service key classes. YOLOv8 achieves 82.5% mAP. Open-source SkeySpot toolkit.
method_summary: Pretrained YOLOv8 object detection on DELP dataset for automated symbol identification, reducing reliance on proprietary CAD systems.

title: MuraNet: Multi-task Floor Plan Recognition with Relation Attention
abstract: Unified multi-task model combining detection and segmentation for floor plan recognition. MURA encoder backbone with enhanced segmentation decoder and decoupled YOLOX-based detection head. Joint training improves feature extraction on CubiCasa5k.
method_summary: Combined detection and segmentation through unified encoder with two specialized branches, leveraging structural relationships between walls, doors, and windows.

title: MitUNet: Enhancing Floor Plan Recognition using a Hybrid Mix-Transformer and U-Net Architecture
abstract: Hybrid neural network combining Mix-Transformer encoder with U-Net decoder featuring spatial and channel attention for wall segmentation. Optimized with Tversky loss. Tested on CubiCasa5k for 3D reconstruction from 2D floor plans.
method_summary: Mix-Transformer encoder paired with enhanced U-Net decoder using spatial and channel attention mechanisms and Tversky loss for accurate wall boundary detection.

title: WAFFLE: Multimodal Floorplan Understanding in the Wild
abstract: Dataset of nearly 20K floorplan images and metadata curated from Internet data spanning diverse building types, locations, and formats. Leverages LLMs and multimodal foundation models for semantic extraction from noisy metadata.
method_summary: Internet-sourced floorplan data combined with LLM and multimodal foundation models for semantic curation, supporting classification and generation tasks across diverse building types.

title: Multi-Unit Floor Plan Recognition and Reconstruction Using Improved Semantic Segmentation of Raster-Wise Floor Plans
abstract: Synthesizes 3D information from 2D architectural floor plans for digital twin creation. Two novel pixel-wise segmentation methods based on MDA-Unet and MACU-Net with improved skip connections, attention mechanism, and vectorization pipeline for 3D model generation. F1 score of 0.86 across five classes.
method_summary: Enhanced semantic segmentation networks (MDA-Unet, MACU-Net) with improved skip connections and attention, followed by reconstruction converting segmented floor plans into vectorized 3D models.

title: Text-to-Layout: A Generative Workflow for Drafting Architectural Floor Plans Using LLMs
abstract: Uses LLMs to generate schematic floor plans from natural language descriptions. Produces layouts with walls, doors, windows, and furniture. Combines prompt engineering, furniture placement refinement algorithm, and Python scripting for Autodesk Revit and BIM compatibility.
method_summary: Prompt engineering paired with furniture placement refinement algorithm and Python scripting, generating spatially coherent draft plans from text with Revit parametric attributes.

title: GraphRAG for Engineering Diagrams
abstract: Combines LLMs with Retrieval-Augmented Generation and knowledge graphs for interacting with engineering diagrams such as P&IDs. Enables natural language querying of diagram content through graph-structured retrieval.
method_summary: Constructs knowledge graphs from digitized engineering diagrams, then uses GraphRAG to enable LLM-based natural language interaction with diagram content for maintenance and operational queries.

title: A Closed-Loop, Physics-Aware Agentic Framework for Auto-Generating Chemical Process and Instrumentation Diagrams
abstract: Physics-aware agentic framework for automatically generating PFDs and P&IDs while adhering to engineering constraints. Closed-loop design with verification against physical laws and engineering standards.
method_summary: Multi-agent system with physics-aware constraints for iterative P&ID generation, incorporating thermodynamic and process engineering validation in the generation loop.

title: Accelerating Manufacturing Scale-Up from Material Discovery Using Agentic Web Navigation and Retrieval-Augmented AI for Process Engineering Schematics Design
abstract: Uses agentic web navigation and RAG for process engineering schematics design, accelerating manufacturing scale-up from material discovery to PFD/PID generation.
method_summary: Agentic AI system combining web navigation for knowledge retrieval with RAG-based generation of process engineering schematics, bridging material discovery and manufacturing design.

title: Unified Vector Floorplan Generation via Markup Representation
abstract: Proposes a unified approach to residential floorplan generation using markup representation, bridging architecture and computer graphics for more efficient spatial design.
method_summary: Markup-based representation unifying different floorplan elements into a single generative framework, enabling end-to-end vector floorplan synthesis from constraints.

title: A Two-Level Codebook Based Network for End-to-End Vector Floorplan Generation
abstract: Two-level codebook network for automated floorplan generation, jointly modeling global spatial organization and precise geometric detail for end-to-end vector output.
method_summary: Hierarchical VQ-VAE with two-level codebooks: one for global room layout topology and one for fine-grained geometric details, enabling end-to-end vector floorplan generation.
